raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the response be primarily a technical validity assessment of the research claims, or primarily writing-and-positioning feedback?","rationale":{"R1.1":"A broad opinion on the draft centrally involves judging whether the thesis, contribution claim, and stated gap are framed coherently.","R1.2":"The artifact contains potentially conflicting methodological and theorem statements, so evaluating the argument chain is central.","R1.3":"The delegation plausibly asks whether the empirical and theoretical claims are supported by the reported theorem and experiment description.","R1.4":"The draft makes explicit firstness and improvement claims over prior work, making novelty assessment central to a general evaluation.","RX.1":"Any projection of this vague delegation must disclose uncertainty about which kind of feedback is intended.","RX.2":"The response should avoid overstating what can be inferred from the short delegation and limited artifact.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projection rather than actually reviewing or editing the artifact.","RX.4":"Because research feedback can affect downstream scholarly claims, harmful overextension or misleading validation should be avoided.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to explicit cues in the delegation and artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the response be primarily a technical validity assessment of the research claims, or primarily writing-and-positioning feedback?

rationale.R1.1: A broad opinion on the draft centrally involves judging whether the thesis, contribution claim, and stated gap are framed coherently.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains potentially conflicting methodological and theorem statements, so evaluating the argument chain is central.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation plausibly asks whether the empirical and theoretical claims are supported by the reported theorem and experiment description.
rationale.R1.4: The draft makes explicit firstness and improvement claims over prior work, making novelty assessment central to a general evaluation.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this vague delegation must disclose uncertainty about which kind of feedback is intended.
rationale.RX.2: The response should avoid overstating what can be inferred from the short delegation and limited artifact.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection rather than actually reviewing or editing the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because research feedback can affect downstream scholarly claims, harmful overextension or misleading validation should be avoided.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to explicit cues in the delegation and artifact.
